featuredStory.title: The Zurich Passage
featuredStory.excerpt: Six hours, three meetings, one unseen hand. You land at 08:00. Your first meeting is at 09:30 in Paradeplatz. Between them: immigration, traffic, check-in, a shower, a pressed shirt, and breakfast. Most executives lose the morning to logistics. Our guest did not.

stories.title: Crossing the Atlantic While You Sleep
stories.excerpt: Depart Teterboro at 21:00 after your New York dinner. At 08:00 London time, you step into a car at Farnborough. The Atlantic crossed itself. You simply slept.

stories.title: The Desert Crossing
stories.excerpt: From Dubai to the Empty Quarter. Three hours by private car. The city dissolves into sand. The last fifteen minutes on unmarked roads. Then: a camp that should not exist this far out.

stories.title: Tokyo to Kyoto by Whisper
stories.excerpt: The Shinkansen is efficient. But efficiency is not always the point. Our guest took the slower train. Stopped at a temple nobody visits. Arrived in Kyoto two hours later than planned. She says it was the best two hours of the year.

stories.title: The Vienna Interval
stories.excerpt: Four hours between flights. Most people wait in lounges. Our guest asked for something else. Within twenty minutes, a car took her to a private wine cellar beneath the city. She returned to the airport carrying a bottle from 1789.
